texto: [H]e quoted eloquently from the Bill of Rights, the Constitution, the Gettysburg Address, and a section which had been stricken from his party's platform seventy-five years ago. He was not quite clear on what all this had to do with [the present situation], but it was noble and stirring and would bring in a lot of votes.
explicacao: Citação de Mark Clifton sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “[H]e quoted eloquently from the Bill of Rights, the Constitution, the Gettysburg Address, and a s…” articula reflexao, grandstanding, patriotism em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como patriotism e politics.

frase_original: [H]e quoted eloquently from the Bill of Rights, the Constitution, the Gettysburg Address, and a section which had been stricken from his party's platform seventy-five years ago. He was not quite clear on what all this had to do with [the present situation], but it was noble and stirring and would bring in a lot of votes.
